feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: Building AlphaGo vs. Mastering Go: The AI Replacement Boundary

link.title: Building AlphaGo vs. Mastering Go: The AI Replacement Boundary

entry.content:
A question I can’t stop thinking about lately: Should we continue to focus on mastering Go as human players, or step back and build AlphaGo, so that humans no longer need to play Go at all? Are there any tasks that AI cannot replace humans in?

To answer this question, I will attempt to replace as much of my daily work as possible with AI and explore the current boundaries. Although AI will undoubtedly become stronger in the future, I want to probe the current limits now, so that I don’t continue to cultivate skills that may soon become useless.
entry.summary: Should we focus on mastering Go as human players, or step back and build AlphaGo? I'm exploring the boundaries of AI replacement in daily work to avoid cultivating obsolete skills.

entry.title: Why Rebuild General Agents? Introducing pycursor_agent

link.title: Why Rebuild General Agents? Introducing pycursor_agent

entry.content:
We’re already paying for Cursor. Cursor already has a pretty capable agent at a competitive price.

So why are we still rebuilding general agents with expensive API keys and complex workflows? 🤔

I hacked a tiny package called pycursor_agent. It lets you invoke Cursor Agent directly from Python, so you can reuse Cursor’s agent capabilities in scripts or pipelines. 🚀

It literally took about 5 minutes to build with Cursor. It hasn’t been heavily tested yet and may have bugs, but it’s already usable and might spark some ideas. 🧪

🔗 GitHub: https://github.com/linwk20/pycursor_agent

Curious if others are doing similar hacks around Cursor — for example, for multi-agent orchestration 🤖
entry.summary: Why rebuild general agents when Cursor already provides a capable one? I built `pycursor_agent` to invoke Cursor's agent directly from Python scripts and pipelines.

entry.title: Hello World
entry.content:
Welcome to my new blog! This is a sample post.

You can edit this in _posts/2026-01-02-hello-world.md.

check out code:

print("Hello World")
entry.summary: First post on the new blog!
